final_answer: To pinpoint the cause of the intermittent 502/504 errors, first collect gateway logs and match error timestamps with backend request IDs (N1:1:80f5b1d1, N6:0:be42ea7d). Enable request‑level tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry) on the gateway and backend to correlate each request with downstream latency (N1:0:73b6996b, N6:1:9c8f589f). Use the tracing data to identify which downstream call or DB query exceeds its SLA. On the database side, activate pg_stat_statements and DB latency/lock‑wait metrics to surface the top CPU‑intensive queries (N2:0:84193441, N7:0:e3ef8b38), then inspect their execution plans for full scans or missing indexes (N7:1:3655c323) and add appropriate indexes (N2:2:61351358). Reduce contention by limiting parallelism or batching DB requests (N6:5:2ab64944). With the latency profile, budget the total request timeout by allocating time slices to each downstream call plus a safety margin (N10:4:c70e96aa) and enforce timeouts and circuit‑breaker thresholds around those calls to fail fast and prevent cascading failures (N1:5:ceffd77b). This systematic tracing, DB query optimization, and timeout/breaker tuning will isolate the bottleneck and stabilize the system.
